claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child. Their name is Alex and they're 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex!

To continue assessing your eligibility, I have a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care, meaning do they live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you.

**Is Alex a British citizen, or do they have the right to live in the UK?** (For example, are they a UK national, or do they have settled or pre-settled status, or another form of immigration permission?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

**And what about yourself – are you living in the United Kingdom, and do you have the right to reside here?** (For example, are you a British citizen, or do you have settled/pre-settled status or another form of immigration permission?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Manchester and I'm living in the UK with the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or training, or are they not in education yet given their age (8 years old)?** And also, **is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 8, so they're in primary school. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old)
- ✅ Alex is a British citizen with the right to live in the UK
- ✅ You live in the UK (Manchester) and have the right to reside here
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex as they live with you
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Let me now provide my eligibility assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

- **Alex is under 16** – At 8 years old, Alex meets the age requirement
- **You are responsible for Alex** – Alex lives with you at home
- **You both have the right to reside in the UK** – You live in Manchester with the right to reside, and Alex is a British citizen
- **No duplicate claims** – No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions about the application process or anything else, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old), which meets the age requirement. (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. (3) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK with the right to reside – the claimant lives in Manchester and Alex is a British citizen. (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim for a child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, both have the right to reside in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old), which meets the age requirement. (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. (3) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK with the right to reside – the claimant lives in Manchester and Alex is a British citizen. (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim for a child.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, both have the right to reside in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child.
